events.title: Aztec Empire at height
events.description: The Aztec Triple Alliance controls central Mexico with complex tribute systems, urban centers, and religious institutions prior to Spanish conquest.

events.title: Aztec Empire at height
events.description: The Aztec Triple Alliance controls central Mexico with complex tribute systems, urban centers, and religious institutions prior to Spanish conquest.

events.title: Ming maritime and economic shifts
events.description: Ming China experiences internal economic growth, maritime trade with Southeast Asia, and cultural developments despite earlier retrenchment.

events.title: Ottoman consolidation in the Balkans
events.description: Ottoman state consolidates control across the Balkans and Anatolia, integrating diverse populations and administrative systems under the sultanate.

events.title: Babur's campaigns in India (precursor to Mughal Empire)
events.description: Babur defeats Ibrahim Lodi at Panipat (1526) and establishes the Mughal presence in northern India, founding a dynasty that will shape South Asian history.

events.title: Spanish incursions into Mesoamerica
events.description: Hernán Cortés leads Spanish forces and indigenous allies to the fall of Tenochtitlan, ending Aztec imperial rule and initiating colonial New Spain.

events.title: Martin Luther posts Ninety-Five Theses
events.description: Martin Luther's theses challenge Catholic practices and spark the Protestant Reformation, reshaping European religion and politics.

events.title: Spanish conquest of the Inca Empire
events.description: Francisco Pizarro captures Atahualpa and topples the Inca state, enabling Spanish colonization of Andean South America.

events.title: Akbar the Great consolidates Mughal rule
events.description: Akbar expands and consolidates the Mughal Empire in India, promotes administrative reforms, religious accommodation, and cultural synthesis.

events.title: Copernicus publishes heliocentric model
events.description: Nicolaus Copernicus publishes De revolutionibus orbium coelestium, proposing a heliocentric model that challenges geocentric cosmology.

events.title: Suleiman the Magnificent's reign begins (early 16th century apex)
events.description: Under Suleiman the Magnificent the Ottoman Empire reaches territorial, legal, and cultural heights across Europe, Asia, and Africa.

events.title: Warring States period and unification efforts in Japan
events.description: Japan's Sengoku (Warring States) era sees daimyo conflict and eventual unification under leaders like Oda Nobunaga, Toyotomi Hideyoshi, and Tokugawa Ieyasu.

events.title: Columbian Exchange begins
events.description: Biological and cultural exchanges between Old and New Worlds transform diets, economies, and ecosystems through crops, animals, and diseases.

events.title: Portuguese establish foothold in India (Goa)
events.description: Portuguese capture Goa and establish a colonial and trading base on the west coast of India, initiating European colonial presence in South Asia.

events.title: Safavid dynasty establishes Shi'a state in Persia
events.description: Ismail I founds the Safavid dynasty, establishing Twelver Shi'ism as the state religion and shaping Persian identity and geopolitics.

events.title: Religious and confessional conflicts begin to reshape Europe
events.description: The Protestant Reformation and Catholic responses lead to religious fragmentation, wars, and political realignments across Europe.

events.title: Mughal administrative and cultural consolidation
events.description: Under Akbar and successors the Mughal state develops centralized administration, revenue systems, and a syncretic court culture blending Persian, Indian, and Central Asian elements.

events.title: European maritime empires expand
events.description: Spain, Portugal, England, and France establish overseas colonies and trade networks in the Americas, Africa, and Asia, reshaping global connections.
